---
title: "Riemann Sum gif"
format: html
---


```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.animation as animation

# ---- function to integrate ----
def f(t):
    return 400 * (t / 60)**5 * (1 - t / 60)**2

a, b = 0, 60
t_smooth = np.linspace(a, b, 2000)
y_smooth = f(t_smooth)
y_max = y_smooth.max()

# ---- number of rectangles per frame ----
# start coarse, grow quickly at first then more slowly (looks smoother as an animation)
n_values = np.unique(np.round(np.geomspace(4, 300, 45)).astype(int))
# hold on the last (finest) frame a little longer
n_values = np.concatenate([n_values, np.full(10, n_values[-1])])

# ---- set up figure ----
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4.5), dpi=120)

def draw_frame(n):
    ax.clear()

    # midpoint Riemann sum
    edges = np.linspace(a, b, n + 1)
    width = edges[1] - edges[0]
    mids = (edges[:-1] + edges[1:]) / 2
    heights = f(mids)
    area = np.sum(heights * width)

    # curve
    ax.plot(t_smooth, y_smooth, color='tab:blue', linewidth=2, zorder=3)

    # rectangles
    ax.bar(mids, heights, width=width, align='center',
           color='tab:blue', alpha=0.35, edgecolor='white',
           linewidth=0.6, zorder=2)

    ax.set_xlim(a, b)
    ax.set_ylim(0, y_max * 1.15)
    ax.set_xlabel('t')
    ax.set_ylabel('f(t)')
    ax.set_title(f'Riemann Sum Approximation of Integral (n = {n} rectangles)')

    ax.text(0.03, 0.92, f'Area ≈ {area:.2f}',
            transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=13, fontweight='bold',
            color='tab:blue',
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.3', facecolor='white',
                      edgecolor='tab:blue', alpha=0.9))

    # clean look: no gray background, minimal spines
    ax.set_facecolor('white')
    fig.patch.set_facecolor('white')
    for spine in ['top', 'right']:
        ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)

    return []

anim = animation.FuncAnimation(
    fig, draw_frame, frames=n_values, blit=False
)

anim.save('riemann_sums.gif', writer='pillow', fps=2)
print("done")

```
